content:
# Numbers Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 145–147; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 110–111 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_001_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_001_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_001_page_003.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_001_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_001_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE CENSUS IN THE WILDERNESS OF SINAI:** The Leningrad Codex opens the book of Numbers (בְּמִדְבַּר, "In the wilderness") on page 145. The Hebrew title derives from the first significant word of 1:1: בְּמִדְבַּר (be-midbar, "in the wilderness"). The Greek title Ἀριθμοί ("Numbers") and its Latin equivalent Numeri derive from the census that dominates this opening chapter. YHWH commands Moses and Aaron to count every male of Israel aged twenty and upward, all who go forth to the host (כָּל־יֹצֵא צָבָא, kol-yotse' tsava'). The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 110–111, heading visible as CAP. X = Num 1 in LXX pagination) follows the MT closely throughout, with the standard rendering ἐπισκέψασθε ("muster/visit") for the Hebrew פקד. The tribal census list (vv. 20–43) follows a rigid formulaic pattern; the LXX reproduces this pattern faithfully. Notable: the LXX consistently renders מַטֶּה ("tribe/staff") with φυλή, and נָשִׂיא ("leader/chieftain") with ἄρχων.

---

## YHWH Commands the Census (vv. 1–4)

**1** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto Moses [el-Mosheh] in-[the]-wilderness of [be-midbar] Sinai [Sinai], in-[the]-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed], on-the-first [be-ekhad] of-the-second [la-khodesh ha-sheni] month [khodesh], in-the-second [ba-shanah ha-shenit] year [shanah] of-their-going-out [le-tse'tam] from-[the]-land of [me-erets] Egypt [Mitsrayim], saying [le'mor]:

> **Key terms:** בְּמִדְבַּר (be-midbar) = "in the wilderness of" — the opening word gives the Hebrew name of the book. בְּאֹהֶל מוֹעֵד (be-ohel mo'ed) = "in the tent of meeting" — the specific location of revelation. **LXX:** ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ τῇ Σινα ("in the wilderness of Sinai") ... ἐν τῇ σκηνῇ τοῦ μαρτυρίου ("in the tent of the testimony") — Vaticanus renders מוֹעֵד as μαρτύριον ("testimony") rather than "meeting."

**2** "Lift up [se'u] the head [et-rosh] of-all [kol] the-congregation of [adat] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam], by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam], by-[the]-number of [be-mispar] names [shemot], every male [kol-zakhar] by-their-heads [le-gulgelotam]."

> **Key terms:** שְׂאוּ אֶת־רֹאשׁ (se'u et-rosh) = "lift up the head" — the idiom for taking a census (lit. "raise the head/sum"). לְגֻלְגְּלֹתָם (le-gulgelotam) = "by their skulls/heads" — individual head-count, from גֻּלְגֹּלֶת ("skull"). **LXX:** λάβετε ἀρχήν ("take a beginning/sum") — a different idiom; Vaticanus renders גֻּלְגֹּלֶת with κατὰ κεφαλήν ("by head").

**3** "From-a-son of [mi-ben] twenty [esrim] year[s] [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah], all [kol] going-out [yotse'] to-the-host [tsava'] in-Israel [be-Yisra'el] — you shall muster them [tifqedu otam] by-their-hosts [le-tsiv'otam], you [attah] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon]."

> **Key terms:** יֹצֵא צָבָא (yotse' tsava') = "going out to the host" — i.e., eligible for military service. תִּפְקְדוּ (tifqedu) = "you shall muster/number" — from פקד, the governing verb of the chapter. **LXX:** ἐπισκέψασθε αὐτούς ("visit/muster them") — from ἐπισκέπτομαι, the standard LXX equivalent of פקד.

**4** "And-with you [ve-ittkhem] there-shall-be [yihyu] a-man [ish], a-man [ish] for-[each]-tribe [la-matteh], a-man [ish] — head of [rosh] the-house of [le-veit] his-fathers [avotav] he [hu']."

> **Key terms:** מַטֶּה (matteh) = "tribe" (lit. "staff/rod"). רֹאשׁ לְבֵית אֲבֹתָיו (rosh le-veit avotav) = "head of the house of his fathers" — the tribal chieftain. **LXX:** φυλῆς ("tribe") ... ἄρχων ("ruler").

---

## The Tribal Leaders Named (vv. 5–16)

**5** And-these [ve-elleh] are the-names of [shemot] the-men [ha-anashim] who [asher] shall-stand [ya'amdu] with you [ittkhem]: For-Reuben [li-Re'uven] — Elizur [Elitsur] son of [ben] Shedeur [Shedei'ur].

> **LXX:** Ελισουρ υἱὸς Σεδιουρ.

**6** For-Simeon [le-Shim'on] — Shelumiel [Shelumi'el] son of [ben] Zurishaddai [Tsuri-shaddai].

**7** For-Judah [li-Yhudah] — Nahshon [Nakhshon] son of [ben] Amminadab [Amminadav].

**8** For-Issachar [le-Yissakhar] — Nethanel [Netan'el] son of [ben] Zuar [Tsu'ar].

**9** For-Zebulun [li-Zvulun] — Eliab [Eli'av] son of [ben] Helon [Khelon].

**10** For-the-sons of [li-vnei] Joseph [Yosef]: For-Ephraim [le-Efrayim] — Elishama [Elishama'] son of [ben] Ammihud [Ammihud]. For-Manasseh [li-Mnasheh] — Gamaliel [Gamli'el] son of [ben] Pedahzur [Pedahtsur].

**11** For-Benjamin [le-Vinyamin] — Abidan [Avidan] son of [ben] Gideoni [Gid'oni].

**12** For-Dan [le-Dan] — Ahiezer [Akhi'ezer] son of [ben] Ammishaddai [Ammi-shaddai].

**13** For-Asher [le-Asher] — Pagiel [Pag'i'el] son of [ben] Ochran [Okhran].

**14** For-Gad [le-Gad] — Eliasaph [Elyasaf] son of [ben] Deuel [De'u'el].

> **Key term:** דְּעוּאֵל (De'u'el) — the Leningrad Codex reads דעואל here. In 2:14 the name appears as רְעוּאֵל (Re'u'el), a well-known dalet/resh variant. **LXX:** Ελισαφ υἱὸς Ραγουηλ — Vaticanus reads Ραγουηλ (= Re'u'el), aligning with the variant.

**15** For-Naphtali [le-Naftali] — Ahira [Akhira'] son of [ben] Enan [Einan].

**16** These [elleh] are the-called-ones of [qeru'ei] the-congregation [ha-edah], leaders of [nesi'ei] the-tribes of [mattot] their-fathers [avotam], heads of [rashei] the-thousands of [alfei] Israel [Yisra'el] they [hem].

> **Key terms:** קְרוּאֵי הָעֵדָה (qeru'ei ha-edah) = "the called-ones of the congregation" — the summoned representatives. נְשִׂיאֵי (nesi'ei) = "leaders/chieftains" — from נשׂא ("to lift up"). **LXX:** ἄρχοντες ("rulers") ... χιλίαρχοι ("commanders of thousands").

---

## Moses and Aaron Muster the Assembly (vv. 17–19)

**17** And-took [va-yiqqakh] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] these [et-ha-anashim ha-elleh] men [ha-anashim] who [asher] were designated [niqqevu] by-names [be-shemot].

> **Key term:** נִקְּבוּ (niqqevu) = "were designated/specified" — from נקב ("to pierce/specify by name"). **LXX:** ἀνελάβοσαν ("they took up").

**18** And-all [ve-et kol] the-congregation [ha-edah] they-assembled [hiqhilu] on-the-first [be-ekhad] of-the-second [la-khodesh ha-sheni] month [khodesh], and-they-registered-their-lineage [va-yityaledu] by-their-clans [al-mishpekhotam], by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam], by-[the]-number of [be-mispar] names [shemot], from-a-son of [mi-ben] twenty [esrim] year[s] [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah], by-their-heads [le-gulgelotam].

> **Key term:** וַיִּתְיַלְדוּ (va-yityaledu) = "and they registered their lineage" — hithpael of ילד ("to beget"); reflexive sense of declaring one's birth-descent. **LXX:** ἐπηξονίσθησαν ("they were enrolled").

**19** As [ka'asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh], and-he-mustered them [va-yifqedem] in-[the]-wilderness of [be-midbar] Sinai [Sinai].

> **LXX:** καὶ ἐπεσκέπησαν ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ τῇ Σινα ("and they were mustered in the wilderness of Sinai").

---

## The Tribal Census (vv. 20–43)

*Each tribe follows a fixed formula: "And-the-sons of [tribe] were — their-begettings [toledotam], by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam], by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam], by-[the]-number of [be-mispar] names [shemot], by-their-heads [le-gulgelotam], every male [kol-zakhar] from-a-son of twenty year[s] and-upward, all going-out to-the-host — their-mustered-ones [pequdeihem] for-the-tribe of [le-matteh] [tribe], [number]." The LXX renders each uniformly with ἐπίσκεψις ("mustering"). EMET preserves the full formula for the first tribe (Reuben), then presents the remaining tribes in condensed form.*

**20–21** And-the-sons of [va-yihyu benei] Reuben [Re'uven], firstborn of [bekhor] Israel [Yisra'el] — their-begettings [toledotam], by-their-clans [le-mishpekhotam], by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam], by-[the]-number of [be-mispar] names [shemot], by-their-heads [le-gulgelotam], every [kol] male [zakhar] from-a-son of [mi-ben] twenty [esrim] year[s] [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah], all [kol] going-out [yotse'] to-the-host [tsava'] — **21** their-mustered-ones [pequdeihem] for-the-tribe of [le-matteh] Reuben [Re'uven]: six [shishah] and-forty [ve-arba'im] thousand [elef] and-five hundred [va-khamesh me'ot].

> **Key terms:** תּוֹלְדֹתָם (toledotam) = "their begettings/generations" — the genealogical registry. פְּקוּדֵיהֶם (pequdeihem) = "their mustered-ones" — passive participle of פקד; the census total. **LXX:** ἡ ἐπίσκεψις αὐτῶν ("their visitation/mustering") — 46,500.

**22–23** For-the-sons of Simeon [Shim'on] — their-mustered-ones: nine [tishah] and-fifty [va-khamishim] thousand [elef] and-three hundred [u-shelosh me'ot]. **= 59,300**

**24–25** For-the-sons of Gad — their-mustered-ones: five [khamishah] and-forty [ve-arba'im] thousand [elef] and-six [ve-shesh] hundred [me'ot] and-fifty [va-khamishim]. **= 45,650**

**26–27** For-the-sons of Judah [Yhudah] — their-mustered-ones: four [arba'ah] and-seventy [ve-shiv'im] thousand [elef] and-six [va-shesh] hundred [me'ot]. **= 74,600**

**28–29** For-the-sons of Issachar [Yissakhar] — their-mustered-ones: four [arba'ah] and-fifty [va-khamishim] thousand [elef] and-four [ve-arba'] hundred [me'ot]. **= 54,400**

**30–31** For-the-sons of Zebulun [Zvulun] — their-mustered-ones: seven [shiv'ah] and-fifty [va-khamishim] thousand [elef] and-four [ve-arba'] hundred [me'ot]. **= 57,400**

**32–33** For-the-sons of Ephraim [Efrayim], son of Joseph [Yosef] — their-mustered-ones: forty [arba'im] thousand [elef] and-five [va-khamesh] hundred [me'ot]. **= 40,500**

**34–35** For-the-sons of Manasseh [Mnasheh], son of Joseph [Yosef] — their-mustered-ones: two [shenayim] and-thirty [u-sheloshim] thousand [elef] and-two hundred [u-matayim]. **= 32,200**

**36–37** For-the-sons of Benjamin [Vinyamin] — their-mustered-ones: five [khamishah] and-thirty [u-sheloshim] thousand [elef] and-four [ve-arba'] hundred [me'ot]. **= 35,400**

**38–39** For-the-sons of Dan — their-mustered-ones: two [shenayim] and-sixty [ve-shishim] thousand [elef] and-seven [va-sheva'] hundred [me'ot]. **= 62,700**

**40–41** For-the-sons of Asher — their-mustered-ones: one [ekhad] and-forty [ve-arba'im] thousand [elef] and-five [va-khamesh] hundred [me'ot]. **= 41,500**

**42–43** For-the-sons of Naphtali [Naftali] — their-mustered-ones: three [sheloshah] and-fifty [va-khamishim] thousand [elef] and-four [ve-arba'] hundred [me'ot]. **= 53,400**

> **LXX note on tribal order:** Vaticanus follows the MT order throughout the census. All twelve numbers match between MT and LXX.

---

## The Census Total (vv. 44–46)

**44** These [elleh] are the-mustered-ones [ha-pequdim] whom [asher] mustered [paqad] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] and-the-leaders of [u-nesi'ei] Israel [Yisra'el] — twelve [shenem asar] men [ish], a-man one [ish-ekhad] for-the-house of [le-veit] his-fathers [avotav] they-were [hayu].

**45** And-they-were [va-yihyu] all [kol] the-mustered-ones of [pequdei] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], by-the-house of [le-veit] their-fathers [avotam], from-a-son of [mi-ben] twenty [esrim] year[s] [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah], all [kol] going-out [yotse'] to-the-host [tsava'] in-Israel [be-Yisra'el] —

**46** and-they-were [va-yihyu] all [kol] the-mustered-ones [ha-pequdim]: six hundred [shesh-me'ot] thousand [elef] and-three [u-sheloshet] thousands [alafim] and-five [va-khamesh] hundred [me'ot] and-fifty [va-khamishim]. **= 603,550**

> **Key term:** שֵׁשׁ־מֵאוֹת אֶלֶף וּשְׁלֹשֶׁת אֲלָפִים וַחֲמֵשׁ מֵאוֹת וַחֲמִשִּׁים = 603,550. **LXX:** ἑξακόσιαι χιλιάδες καὶ τρισχίλιοι καὶ πεντακόσιοι καὶ πεντήκοντα — identical total.

---

## The Levites Exempted (vv. 47–51)

**47** And-the-Levites [ve-ha-Levi'im], for-the-tribe of [le-matteh] their-fathers [avotam], were-not [lo'] mustered [hotpaqedu] in-their-midst [be-tokham].

> **Key term:** לֹא הָתְפָּקְדוּ (lo' hotpaqedu) = "were not mustered" — hothpael (passive reflexive) of פקד. **LXX:** οὐ συνεπεσκέπησαν ("were not mustered together").

**48** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto Moses [el-Mosheh] saying [le'mor]:

**49** "Only [akh] the-tribe of [et-matteh] Levi [Levi] you-shall-not [lo'] muster [tifqod], and their-head [ve-et-rosham] you-shall-not [lo'] lift up [tissa'] in-the-midst of [be-tokh] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el]."

> **Key terms:** אַךְ (akh) = "only/surely" — a restrictive particle emphasizing the Levitical exemption. לֹא תִשָּׂא (lo' tissa') = "you shall not lift up" — i.e., do not count them in the census.

**50** "And-you [ve-attah], appoint [hafqed] the-Levites [et-ha-Levi'im] over [al] the-tabernacle of [mishkan] the-testimony [ha-edut] and-over [ve-al] all [kol] its-vessels [kelav] and-over [ve-al] all [kol] that [asher] belongs-to-it [lo]. They [hemah] shall-carry [yis'u] the-tabernacle [et-ha-mishkan] and all [ve-et-kol] its-vessels [kelav], and-they [ve-hem] shall-minister-to [yesharetu] it [oto], and-around [saviv] the-tabernacle [la-mishkan] they-shall-encamp [yakhanu]."

> **Key terms:** הַפְקֵד (hafqed) = "appoint" — hiphil imperative of פקד; the same root used for census now means "to appoint/charge over." מִשְׁכַּן הָעֵדֻת (mishkan ha-edut) = "tabernacle of the testimony." יְשָׁרְתוּ (yesharetu) = "they shall minister" — from שׁרת (piel), priestly/Levitical service. **LXX:** λειτουργήσουσιν ("they shall minister") ... τὴν σκηνὴν τοῦ μαρτυρίου ("the tent of the testimony").

**51** "And-in-the-journeying of [u-vin'so'a] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan], the-Levites [ha-Levi'im] shall-take-it-down [yoridu oto], and-in-the-encamping of [u-va-khanot] the-tabernacle [ha-mishkan], the-Levites [ha-Levi'im] shall-raise-it-up [yaqimu oto]. And-the-stranger [ve-ha-zar] who-approaches [ha-qarev] shall-be-put-to-death [yumat]."

> **Key terms:** יוֹרִידוּ (yoridu) = "they shall take it down" — hiphil of ירד ("to go down"); the Levites disassemble the tabernacle. יָקִימוּ (yaqimu) = "they shall raise it up" — hiphil of קום; they reassemble it. הַזָּר הַקָּרֵב יוּמָת (ha-zar ha-qarev yumat) = "the stranger who approaches shall be put to death" — זָר (zar) here means any non-Levite; hophal of מות = "shall surely be put to death." **LXX:** ὁ προσπορευόμενος ὁ ἀλλογενὴς ἀποθανέτω ("the foreigner who approaches, let him die") — ἀλλογενής ("of another race") interprets זָר as ethnic outsider.

---

## The Camp Arrangement and Conclusion (vv. 52–54)

**52** "And-shall-encamp [ve-khanu] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], a-man [ish] upon [al] his-camp [makhaneihu] and-a-man [ve-ish] upon [al] his-standard [diglo], by-their-hosts [le-tsiv'otam]."

> **Key terms:** מַחֲנֵהוּ (makhaneihu) = "his camp" — each tribe's designated encampment. דִּגְלוֹ (diglo) = "his standard/banner" — the tribal ensign around which each group rallies. **LXX:** τάγμα αὐτοῦ ("his division/order") for דִּגְלוֹ.

**53** "And-the-Levites [ve-ha-Levi'im] shall-encamp [yakhanu] around [saviv] the-tabernacle of [le-mishkan] the-testimony [ha-edut], and-there-shall-not-be [ve-lo'-yihyeh] wrath [qetsef] upon [al] the-congregation of [adat] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el]. And-shall-keep [ve-shameru] the-Levites [ha-Levi'im] the-charge of [et-mishmeret] the-tabernacle of [mishkan] the-testimony [ha-edut]."

> **Key terms:** קֶצֶף (qetsef) = "wrath/fury" — divine anger that would strike if non-Levites approach the sacred space. מִשְׁמֶרֶת (mishmeret) = "charge/guard-duty" — from שׁמר ("to keep/guard"); the Levites' protective watch around the tabernacle. **LXX:** ἡ φυλακή ("the guard/watch") ... μὴ γένηται ἁμάρτημα ("lest there be a sin") — Vaticanus renders קֶצֶף as ἁμάρτημα ("sin/offense") rather than "wrath."

**54** And-did [va-ya'asu] the-sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el] according-to-all [ke-khol] that [asher] YHWH commanded [tsivvah] Moses [et-Mosheh] — so [ken] they-did [asu].

> **Key terms:** כֵּן עָשׂוּ (ken asu) = "so they did" — the obedience formula closing the chapter, echoing the singular form (כֵּן עָשָׂה) used of Moses throughout Exodus 39–40. **LXX:** οὕτως ἐποίησαν ("thus they did").

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### 1. The Root פקד — Governing Verb of the Chapter
The root פקד ("to muster/visit/appoint/attend to") occurs more frequently in Numbers 1 than in any other chapter of the Torah. It appears in multiple stems: qal (פָּקַד, "he mustered," v. 44), hiphil (הַפְקֵד, "appoint," v. 50), hothpael (הָתְפָּקְדוּ, "were mustered," v. 47), and as a noun (פְּקוּדִים, "mustered-ones," vv. 21–46). The semantic range of פקד — simultaneously "to count," "to attend to," "to appoint," and "to visit" — reveals that census-taking in Israel was not merely administrative but covenantal: YHWH "visits" His people by counting them. The LXX captures this with ἐπισκέπτομαι ("to look upon, visit, inspect"), the root of "bishop" (ἐπίσκοπος).

### 2. שְׂאוּ אֶת־רֹאשׁ — "Lift Up the Head"
The census idiom (v. 2) literally means "raise the head/sum." The same phrase appears in Genesis 40:13, 19–20 where it means both "lift up the head" (restoration) and "lift up the head from upon you" (execution). In Numbers, the idiom is administrative — to count by "raising" each individual into visibility. The LXX's λάβετε ἀρχήν ("take a sum/beginning") loses the bodily metaphor.

### 3. לְגֻלְגְּלֹתָם — "By Their Skulls"
The term גֻּלְגֹּלֶת (gulgolet, v. 2) = "skull" is the basis for individual head-counting. The same word gives the place-name Golgotha (Γολγοθᾶ) in the NT (Matt 27:33). Here it emphasizes that every individual male was counted one by one — a "poll" in the original sense.

### 4. וַיִּתְיַלְדוּ — "They Registered Their Lineage" (v. 18)
The hithpael of ילד ("to beget") is rare and striking. The reflexive form means "they declared their birth-lineage" — each man identified himself by patrilineal descent. This is the genealogical act that undergirds the entire census structure: identity in Israel is established through father's house (בֵּית אָב).

### 5. The Deuel/Reuel Variant (v. 14)
The Leningrad Codex reads דְּעוּאֵל (De'u'el, "knowledge of God") in 1:14, but רְעוּאֵל (Re'u'el, "friend of God") in 2:14 for the same person — the father of Eliasaph, leader of Gad. The confusion arises from the graphical similarity of dalet (ד) and resh (ר) in the Hebrew square script. The LXX consistently reads Ραγουηλ (= Re'u'el) throughout, suggesting its Vorlage may have had resh in both places.

### 6. The Levitical Exemption and the זָר (vv. 47–53)
The Levites are excluded from the military census (v. 47) and given a distinct role: guardians of the mishkan ha-edut ("tabernacle of the testimony"). The term זָר (zar, v. 51) does not mean "foreigner" in the ethnic sense but "unauthorized person" — any non-Levite who encroaches on the sacred precincts. The penalty is death (יוּמָת, yumat — hophal of מות). The LXX's ἀλλογενής ("one of another race") shifts the meaning toward ethnic exclusion, which slightly misrepresents the MT's cultic-functional sense.

### 7. דֶּגֶל — "Standard/Banner" (v. 52)
The term דֶּגֶל (degel) appears here for the first time in the Torah, introducing the camp-arrangement system that will be detailed in chapter 2. Each tribal group has its own banner. The LXX renders this as τάγμα ("order/division"), prioritizing the organizational sense over the physical object. The word appears in Song of Songs 2:4 ("his banner over me is love"), showing its range from military to intimate contexts.

### 8. קֶצֶף — "Wrath" (v. 53)
The Levitical encampment around the tabernacle serves as a buffer to prevent divine קֶצֶף (qetsef, "wrath") from breaking out against the congregation. This theology of sacred space — where proximity to holiness without authorization brings death — is central to Numbers. Vaticanus renders this as ἁμάρτημα ("sin/offense"), shifting from the divine emotion (wrath) to the human cause (sin), a notable interpretive move.

### 9. Census Numbers — Historical and Textual Questions
The total of 603,550 males aged 20+ implies a total population of approximately 2–2.5 million. This figure has generated extensive scholarly discussion. The Hebrew אֶלֶף ('elef) can mean both "thousand" and "clan/military unit." Some scholars propose that the census originally counted clan-units rather than individuals (e.g., Reuben: 46 clans, 500 men). The LXX translates straightforwardly as χιλιάδες ("thousands"), preserving the large numbers without qualification. The Leningrad Codex presents the numbers without ambiguity in standard Hebrew numeral form.

### 10. MT-LXX Agreement
Numbers 1 displays unusually high agreement between the Masoretic Text and the Vaticanus LXX. The tribal order, all twelve census numbers, the total (603,550), and the Levitical exemption passage are virtually identical in both traditions. This stands in contrast to chapters like Exodus 35–40, where significant LXX reordering occurs. The primary divergences are translational rather than text-critical: the rendering of פקד, the treatment of זָר, and the interpretation of קֶצֶף noted above.
